column1: write test cases for mobile number and otp
column34:
h3. Summary

This issue involves writing test cases for validating mobile numbers and OTP functionality.

h3. Context

The mobile number input must adhere to specific criteria to ensure proper functionality.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Accepts 10-digit numeric values only.
* Cannot contain alphabets or special characters.
* Cannot be blank.
* OTP is sent after entering the mobile number.

h3. Other information

h4. OTP Field

* 6-digit numeric.
* Valid for 5 minutes.
* Incorrect OTP → “Invalid OTP.”

column1: create test cases for password text field
column34:
h3. Summary

Create test cases for the password text field to ensure it meets specific requirements.

h3. Context

The password text field must adhere to defined security standards. These standards include character requirements and strength indicators.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Minimum length of 8 characters and a maximum of 20 characters.
* Must include:
** 1 uppercase letter
** 1 lowercase letter
** 1 number
** 1 special character
* Password cannot be blank.
* Display password strength as Weak, Medium, or Strong.
* Accept passwords only when classified as Strong.

h3. Other information

N/A

column34:
h3. Summary

Implement login functionality using social platforms such as Google and Facebook.

h3. Context

The goal is to allow users to log in only through authenticated social accounts. This will streamline the login process and enhance user experience.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Users can log in using Google or Facebook.
* Only authenticated logins are permitted.
* On first login, an auto profile creation occurs.
* If a user tries to log in with an account that already exists, display the message: “Account already exists”.

h3. Other information

N/A

h3. 2.1.1 Email Field

* Same validation as registration
* Not registered → “Email not found”

h3. 2.1.2 Mobile Number Field

* 10-digit numeric only
* Cannot be blank
* Not registered → “Number not found”

h3. 2.1.3 Password Field

* Cannot be blank
* Incorrect → “Incorrect password”

h3. Summary

The issue concerns the login button's function in version 2.1.4.

h3. Context

The login button activates only with valid user inputs.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Login button enables only with valid inputs.
* Clicking logs in the user.
* Invalid inputs show inline errors.

h3. Other information

N/A

h3. Summary

The issue is about the "Forgot Password" feature. It involves sending a reset link or OTP to users.

h3. Context

This feature requires users to have a registered email or mobile number for password recovery.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* The system must send a reset link or OTP.
* Users must have a registered email or mobile number.

h3. Other information

N/A
